title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer requests three changes: (1) update the `CreateImmutableConfigMap` docstring to warn that old hash-suffixed ConfigMaps accumulate and are not cleaned up until the owner is deleted, so callers should prune them; (2) fix a hash collision bug in the ConfigMap content hashing where values with embedded newlines can produce ambiguous encodings—use length-prefixed encoding instead; (3) correct the PR description and reference docs to show `EnsureTLSCertSecret` returns `(bool, error)`, not just `error`.

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]

code_comments.body:
The hash encoding format is ambiguous for ConfigMap values containing embedded newlines. A single-entry ConfigMap with key `"key1"` and value `"x\nb=y"` produces the same hash as a two-entry ConfigMap with entries `key1="x"` and `b="y"`. This matters because OpenStack config values (e.g., full INI blobs) regularly contain newlines, making this a realistic collision rather than theoretical.

The docstring claims the format is "unambiguous" because "ConfigMap keys cannot contain '='", but that doesn't prevent ambiguity when values themselves contain `=` and newlines.

Use length-prefixed encoding to eliminate ambiguity:

```go
h := sha256.New()
for _, k := range keys {
    _, _ = fmt.Fprintf(h, "%d:%s=%d:%s\n", len(k), k, len(data[k]), data[k])
}
```

This makes each entry self-delimiting regardless of content.

code_comments.body:
The function implementation correctly returns `(bool, error)` for readiness signalling, but the PR description's Key Components section documents it as returning just `error`. The function's docstring is correct; update the PR description and `docs/reference/kubernetes-packages.md` to reflect the correct signature `(bool, error)` so that callers understand the function blocks on readiness.
